summary: Independent QA accepts commit 383badb1dfe9ecb24a2e4ac3caffbb0825eaa7db for M5-CLIENT-PLAYABLE-UI-001. The real implementation diff from 03afd416a7d07c1cf1c6c21f84b9c059b2a1ffca to 383badb1 stays inside allowed paths and touches no forbidden sim-core, sim-ai, save-format, desktop, Electron, content-source, tools, roadmap, gate, status, release, manifest, checksum, or validation-report paths. The UI projects the accepted M5 command script and M3/M4 read-model evidence through client-core/UI/Web surfaces, stores only UI session progress plus read-model slices, exposes M5 goal, command preview/confirm/result status, client checkpoint save/load, AI/player reason codes, campaign risk, supply/season, automatic battle/siege/withdrawal evidence, and postwar consequences, and introduces no manual battle UI, telemetry, accounts, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mod, branding, release, or commercial scope.

tests_run.result: Branch feat/m5-client-playable-ui; clean before QA handoff creation. Current HEAD is a6041100 routing-only, with reviewed implementation commit 383badb1 as parent.

Changed files are apps/web shell test/shell/style, packages/client-core, packages/ui, tests, project task/state/message/route files. No packages/sim-core, packages/sim-ai, packages/save-format, apps/desktop, packages/platform-electron, content-source, tools, docs gate/roadmap/status/release forbidden paths are in the implementation diff.

Storybook build passed using the repo's actual script. The existing WebClientShell story is reused; .storybook is outside task allowed_paths. Vite reported the existing large chunk warning.

8 Playwright tests passed, including M5 start-to-finish, save/load, preview/confirm/result status, AI reason display, postwar display, failure/cancel flow, 4000-row stress continuity, and M5 no-horizontal-overflow checks at 1280x720, 1280x800, 1920x1080, and 2560x1080.
Expected script-name check: package.json has no build:storybook script. The actual repo script is storybook:build, which passed.
Expected script-name check: package.json has no e2e script. The actual repo script is test:e2e, which passed.
No business-code or generated-file changes after QA validation before writing this QA handoff.

decisions: ACCEPT: Scope is compliant. The reviewed implementation diff is limited to allowed apps/web, packages/client-core, packages/ui, tests, and project task/message/state paths; forbidden simulation, AI, save-format, desktop/Electron, content-source, tools, gate, roadmap, status, release, manifest, checksum, and validation report paths are absent.

ACCEPT: Client authority boundary is preserved. React state added in packages/ui is limited to M5 UI session progress, preview id, confirmed command ids, client checkpoint text, and status strings; client-core exposes read-model DTOs and GameCommandV1 command DTOs, not authoritative WorldState mutation.

ACCEPT: The UI presents the required M5 surfaces: goal and conditions, command preview/confirm/result status, client checkpoint save/load, AI reason codes, campaign risk, supply and season state, automatic field engagement/siege/withdrawal evidence, postwar consequences, replay evidence, and reason summaries.

ACCEPT: Manual battle and commercial/network scope remain absent. The only manual battle text is an explicit out-of-scope notice; no tactical/manual battle UI, telemetry, account system, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mod, branding, release, or commercial path was introduced.

ACCEPT: Storybook limitation is acceptable for this task. The active Storybook config lives under .storybook, which is outside allowed_paths, so the writer reused the existing WebClientShell story; the updated Web shell includes the M5 workspace and pnpm storybook:build passed.

ACCEPT: Playwright coverage is sufficient for this task's UI risk: M5 start-to-finish, save/load, preview/confirm/result status, AI reason display, postwar display, failure/cancel, large table/map continuity, and supported desktop no-horizontal-overflow checks all ran and passed.

ACCEPT: The task file's stale required command names were handled correctly. The repo has storybook:build and test:e2e, not build:storybook or e2e; actual scripts passed and missing-script checks were recorded as expected.

risks.risk: Storybook coverage is through the existing WebClientShell story rather than a newly added dedicated M5 story file because .storybook is outside this task's allowed_paths.
risks.impact: Acceptable for M5-CLIENT-PLAYABLE-UI-001 because WebClientShell now renders the M5 workspace and the Storybook build passed; future UI-state matrix work may still want dedicated stories when allowed.

risks.risk: The client flow submits GameCommandV1 DTOs and displays protocol/read-model evidence, but does not add a new authoritative browser worker execution adapter for every M5 command.
risks.impact: Acceptable for this client UI task because the accepted M5 sim loop and Node/Worker-compatible determinism evidence remain green, and this task's boundary is the playable protocol/read-model UI surface.

risks.risk: Web and Storybook builds still emit the pre-existing Vite large chunk warning.
risks.impact: Non-blocking; no new dependency or release artifact scope was added.

requested_action: Lead may accept M5-CLIENT-PLAYABLE-UI-001 for integration review. Do not mark ACCEPTED/CLOSED until lead acceptance, PR/CI, and main integration gates complete.
